channel.title: Number Theory on Jasper's Technical Notebook
channel.description: Recent content in Number Theory on Jasper's Technical Notebook

item.title: Number Theory #0: Common Formulae — Legendre's Formula & Divisor Convolutions
item.description: Legendre’s formula, divisor and multiple convolutions in O(n log n), offline-difference batch processing for H(n)=Σf(d)g(⌊n/d⌋).

item.title: Number Theory #1: Elementary Number Theory — GCD, LCM, Euler, CRT
item.description: GCD/LCM properties, Bézout/Frobenius coin problem, Euler’s theorem with power-tower reduction, φ-chain, CRT, congruence system merging, n! prime factorisation.

item.title: Number Theory #1.5: Number-Theoretic (Divisibility) Blocking
item.description: Divisibility blocking for O(sqrt(n)) floor-sum evaluation, single/multi-variable, ceiling-division blocking, with CSES 1082 tutorial.

item.title: Number Theory #2-ex: Möbius Inversion — 7 Core Techniques
item.description: Seven core Möbius inversion trick patterns with worked examples: gcd-enumerating, T=dk product substitution, divisor symmetry, and frequency-based简介 inversion.

item.title: Number Theory #2-ex: Möbius Practice — LCM Sums, Divisor-Count, Omega Numbers
item.description: LCM-sum problems (LCMSUM, Crash’s table, FSF’s game), SDOI2015 divisor-count sum, CF 2176F Omega Numbers — all using Möbius/φ inversion.

item.title: Number Theory #3: From Eratosthenes to Möbius Inclusion-Exclusion
item.description: Computing μ via Eratosthenes, Möbius inclusion-exclusion (multiple-form), coprime counting, CF 803F Coprime Subsequences with both bottom-up and Möbius approaches.

item.title: Number Theory #3~4: Sieve Problems — Coin System DP via Sieve
item.description: Sieve+DP for coin systems with multiple constraints, greedy optimality, transition derived from divisor-level savings.

item.title: Number Theory #4: Min_25 Sieve and Du Jiao Sieve
item.description: Min_25 Sieve for Σ_{p≤x}f(p) in O(n^{3/4}/log n). Du Jiao Sieve for sub-linear prefix sums of multiplicative functions via convolution identities.

item.title: Number Theory #4: Primality, Factorisation & Residues (Miller-Rabin, Pollard Rho, Tonelli-Shanks)
item.description: Miller-Rabin (64-bit deterministic), Pollard Rho factorisation, Tonelli-Shanks quadratic residues, cubic roots, quartic roots, and general k-th/N-th residues.
